development-best-practices.overview: Best practices for implementing features and modernizing Java applications in the Smart SDLC workflow. These guidelines ensure high-quality, maintainable, and secure code that meets enterprise standards.

principle.name: Understand Before Implementing
principle.description: Always fully understand the requirements and existing codebase before making changes. Read the Jira story carefully, examine related code, and clarify ambiguities.
principle.rationale: Rushing into implementation without understanding leads to incorrect solutions, rework, and technical debt.

principle.name: Follow Existing Patterns
principle.description: Maintain consistency with existing code patterns, naming conventions, and architectural decisions unless there's a compelling reason to change.
principle.rationale: Consistency makes code easier to understand, maintain, and review. Mixing patterns creates confusion and maintenance burden.

principle.name: Test-Driven Approach
principle.description: Write tests alongside or before implementation. Ensure all new code has appropriate test coverage.
principle.rationale: Tests catch bugs early, document expected behavior, and enable confident refactoring.

principle.name: Preserve Existing Public Behavior During Modernization
principle.description: For Java modernization tasks, preserve the observable behavior of existing public APIs unless the Jira story or user explicitly requires a behavior change.
principle.rationale: Modernization is primarily a compatibility and maintainability exercise. Changing public behavior during modernization causes avoidable regressions, especially in existing unit tests and downstream callers.

rules.rule: Observable behavior includes thrown exception types, null handling, return values, boolean helper semantics, enum values, enum descriptions, parse behavior, getter names, and repository/service method contracts.
Before changing model, repository, enum, or utility code, inspect the existing tests and preserve their expectations exactly.

Do not "improve" behavior during modernization by adding stricter validation, changing fallback behavior, renaming descriptions, or altering helper method semantics unless requirements explicitly call for it.
If a behavior change is explicitly required, update or add tests in the same change and document the reason in code comments and Jira.
For modernization-only tasks, compatibility with existing tests takes precedence over stylistic refactoring.

principle.name: Incremental Changes
principle.description: Make small, focused changes rather than large, sweeping modifications. Commit frequently with clear messages.
principle.rationale: Small changes are easier to review, test, and debug. They reduce risk and make it easier to identify issues.

principle.name: Document Decisions
principle.description: Document important technical decisions, trade-offs, and non-obvious implementation choices in code comments and Jira.
principle.rationale: Future developers (including yourself) need context to understand why code was written a certain way.

guideline.title: Single Responsibility Principle
guideline.description: Each class should have one clear responsibility
guideline.example: Separate OrderService (business logic) from OrderRepository (data access) from OrderController (HTTP handling)

guideline.title: Dependency Injection
guideline.description: Use constructor injection for required dependencies

guideline.title: Package Structure
guideline.description: Organize code by feature or domain, not by layer
guideline.preferred: com.example.ecommerce/ ├── order/ │ ├── Order.java │ ├── OrderService.java │ ├── OrderRepository.java │ └── OrderController.java ├── payment/ │ ├── Payment.java │ ├── PaymentService.java │ └── PaymentController.java

guideline.title: Use Specific Exceptions
guideline.description: Create custom exceptions for domain-specific errors

guideline.title: Don't Swallow Exceptions
guideline.description: Always handle or propagate exceptions appropriately

guideline.title: Validate Input Early
guideline.description: Validate method parameters at the start of methods for new feature code, service-layer orchestration, and externally facing APIs.
guideline.important: For modernization tasks, do not add new validation to existing public model, repository, enum, or utility APIs unless the original behavior already required it or the Jira story explicitly calls for it. Preserving existing behavior is more important than introducing stricter checks.

guideline.title: Preserve Legacy Exception Contracts
guideline.description: If an existing method historically throws a specific exception type for invalid input, keep that exception type and behavior unless a requirements change explicitly approves a contract change.

rules.rule: Do not replace exception-throwing parse methods with null-returning or default-returning implementations.
Do not silently normalize null, blank, or invalid values if the legacy implementation threw IllegalArgumentException.
Do not change repository null-handling semantics during modernization unless tests and requirements are updated together.

guideline.title: Log Meaningful Information
guideline.description: Include context that helps diagnose issues
guideline.good: logger.info("Processing order {} for customer {}", orderId, customerId); logger.error("Payment failed for order {}: {}", orderId, errorMessage);

guideline.title: Don't Log Sensitive Data
guideline.description: Never log passwords, credit cards, or PII

practice.title: Externalize Configuration
practice.description: Use application.properties or application.yml for configuration

practice.title: Use DTOs for API Contracts
practice.description: Separate domain models from API representations
practice.rationale: Prevents exposing internal structure, allows API evolution, and provides clear API contracts

guidelines.guideline: Use appropriate HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
Use plural nouns for resource names (/orders, /customers)

practice.title: Use Transactions Appropriately
practice.description: Mark service methods with @Transactional when needed

practice.title: Write Unit Tests for Business Logic
practice.description: Test service layer methods with mocked dependencies

practice.title: Test Edge Cases and Error Conditions
practice.description: Don't just test the happy path

practice.title: Use Descriptive Test Names
practice.description: Test names should describe what is being tested and expected outcome

practice.title: Follow AAA Pattern
practice.description: Structure tests with Arrange, Act, Assert sections

practice.title: Maintain Test Independence
practice.description: Each test should be independent and not rely on other tests

guidelines.guideline: Don't share mutable state between tests
Clean up resources in @AfterEach if needed
Tests should pass in any order

practice.title: Use Existing Tests as the Compatibility Oracle During Modernization
practice.description: When modernizing legacy code, existing tests define the current contract unless the requirements explicitly call for a behavior change.

rules.rule: Before changing a domain model, enum, repository, or parser, read its existing tests first.
If tests assert exact exception type, message, enum description, or boolean helper behavior, preserve those expectations exactly.
If modernization intentionally changes behavior, update tests in the same commit and document why.
Do not classify compatibility regressions as "just test failures" and move on.

practice.title: Never Hardcode Credentials
practice.description: Use environment variables or secure configuration management
practice.bad: String apiKey = "sk_live_abc123xyz"; // BAD! String password = "admin123"; // BAD!

practice.title: Validate and Sanitize Input
practice.description: Never trust user input, always validate and sanitize

practice.title: Use Parameterized Queries
practice.description: Prevent SQL injection by using prepared statements
practice.bad: // SQL Injection vulnerability! String query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '" + email + "'";

practice.title: Implement Proper Authentication and Authorization
practice.description: Use Spring Security for authentication and authorization

practice.title: Handle Sensitive Data Carefully
practice.description: Encrypt sensitive data and minimize exposure

guidelines.guideline: Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit
Use HTTPS for all API communications

guideline.title: Upgrade Java Version Incrementally
guideline.description: When modernizing from Java 8 to newer versions

steps.step: Update pom.xml with target Java version
Replace deprecated APIs with modern alternatives
Adopt new language features where beneficial
Run all tests to ensure compatibility

guideline.title: Never Use Preview Features
guideline.description: Preview features are experimental, subject to change, and will cause the application to fail to start in a standard JVM that does not have --enable-preview enabled. Never use preview features in any part of the modernized application.

forbidden-in-java-code.feature: String Templates (JEP 430) — preview in Java 21, use String.format() or formatted() instead
Unnamed Classes and Instance Main Methods (JEP 445) — preview in Java 21
Unnamed Patterns and Variables (JEP 443) — preview in Java 21, use named variables
Scoped Values (JEP 446) — preview in Java 21, use ThreadLocal instead

forbidden-in-pom-xml.item: Do NOT add --enable-preview to maven-compiler-plugin compilerArgs
Do NOT add --enable-preview to maven-surefire-plugin argLine
Example of what NOT to do: <compilerArgs><arg>--enable-preview</arg></compilerArgs>

forbidden-in-dockerfile.item: Do NOT add --enable-preview to the java command in CMD or ENTRYPOINT
Example of what NOT to do: CMD ["java", "--enable-preview", "-jar", "app.jar"]

verification-checklist.check: Search pom.xml for the string "enable-preview" — must not be present
Search Dockerfile for the string "enable-preview" — must not be present
Verify mvn clean package succeeds WITHOUT --enable-preview in any configuration
Verify the application starts with a plain: java -jar app.jar

guideline.title: Refactor Legacy Code Gradually
guideline.description: Don't try to modernize everything at once

guideline.title: Adopt Modern Spring Boot Practices
guideline.description: Update to current Spring Boot patterns

updates.update: Use constructor injection instead of field injection
Replace XML configuration with Java config
Adopt Spring Boot 3.x features if upgrading
Use WebFlux for reactive applications if appropriate

guideline.title: Spring Boot 3.x Swagger / OpenAPI UI Path
guideline.description: Springfox is incompatible with Spring Boot 3.x and must not be used. Use SpringDoc OpenAPI (springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui) instead. The Swagger UI path changed between Spring Boot 2.x and 3.x.

rules.rule: Always use springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui in pom.xml for Spring Boot 3.x. Never use springfox.
Always report the Swagger UI as: <route-url>/swagger-ui/index.html — never /swagger-ui.html.
When updating the Jira ticket or posting deployment results in chat, include the correct Swagger UI URL.

category.name: Functionality
category.item: No obvious bugs or logic errors

category.name: Code Quality
category.item: Methods are focused and not too long

category.name: Testing
category.item: Tests are meaningful and not just for coverage
For modernization, existing tests are treated as compatibility requirements unless explicitly superseded by new requirements.

maven-execution.rule: Always use the system `mvn` command, never `./mvnw`. The Maven wrapper script requires execute permissions that may not be set, and the system Maven (installed via SDKMAN or package manager) is always available in this lab environment.
Do not attempt to chmod or fix ./mvnw permissions. Use `mvn` directly.

jacoco-coverage.rule: Always add the JaCoCo Maven plugin to the modernized pom.xml. Without it, coverage reports cannot be generated and the coverage comparison step of the workflow will fail.
Include this plugin in the modernized pom.xml when generating the project structure — do not wait for the coverage step to discover it is missing.

maven-surefire-java21.rule: When modernizing to Java 21, the Maven Surefire plugin must be explicitly configured with the Java 21 executable path. Without this configuration, tests will fail with "UnsupportedClassVersionError" or similar errors even if JAVA_HOME is set correctly.
Include this plugin configuration in the modernized pom.xml when generating the project structure — do not wait for test failures to discover it is missing.
The jvm parameter must point to ${env.JAVA_HOME}/bin/java to ensure tests run with Java 21, not an older version that might be in the system PATH.
maven-surefire-java21.verification: After adding this configuration, run `mvn clean test` to verify all tests pass with Java 21. If tests still fail, verify JAVA_HOME is set: `echo $JAVA_HOME`

java21-record-test-compatibility.rule: When modernizing domain model classes to Java 21 records, the existing tests were written against the original class API. Maintain backward compatibility so all existing tests continue to pass without modification.
java21-record-test-compatibility.guideline: Add legacy getter methods (getId(), getName(), etc.) alongside record accessor methods (id(), name()). Records do not generate getXxx() methods — add them explicitly.

Do NOT add validation logic inside record compact constructors. Move validation to the service layer. Record constructors with validation break tests that intentionally create invalid objects for negative test cases.

Do NOT convert request/command objects (e.g. CreateOrderRequest) to records if the existing tests use no-arg constructors and setters. Only convert truly immutable domain objects (e.g. Product, OrderItem) to records.
Do NOT add or remove enum values to match a "more complete" model. If the legacy tests assert on a specific set of enum values, preserve that set exactly.
Preserve enum metadata exactly, including descriptions, labels, getDescription(), toString(), and any text rendered to UI or asserted in tests.
Preserve enum parsing semantics exactly. If a legacy fromString(String) method threw IllegalArgumentException for null, blank, whitespace, or invalid input, keep that behavior unchanged.
Do NOT replace exception-throwing parse methods with null-returning or default-returning implementations unless requirements explicitly say so.
Preserve legacy boolean helper semantics exactly, such as isCompleted(), isPending(), isExpired(), or similar status-based methods.
Do NOT introduce Objects.requireNonNull or equivalent strict validation into model or repository APIs if the legacy tests depend on looser null-handling behavior.
java21-record-test-compatibility.verification: After implementation, run `mvn test` and confirm 0 failures before proceeding to SonarQube analysis. If tests fail, fix the compatibility issue before moving on — do not skip or work around failing tests.
java21-record-test-compatibility.focused-verification: After each change to a model, enum, parser, repository, or helper method, run the relevant focused tests for that class before running the full suite.

testing-immutable-records.description: When testing Java 21 records (immutable data classes), traditional test patterns that rely on setters no longer work. Use the "with" pattern to create modified copies for testing state changes.

rules.rule: Always add "with" methods to records when tests need to verify state changes
Never try to modify record fields directly - records are immutable
Use descriptive variable names for each state (pendingOrder, completedOrder)
Test immutability by verifying original instances remain unchanged
Add "with" methods during record creation, not as an afterthought

jackson-serialization.description: Spring Boot projects that include SpringDoc OpenAPI (springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui) can have their shared ObjectMapper's visibility settings overridden by SpringDoc's schema introspection. This causes REST endpoints to return only `isXxx()` boolean properties while all other fields (getId(), getCustomerId(), etc.) are silently omitted from the JSON response.
jackson-serialization.rule: Always generate a JacksonConfig class in the config package that explicitly sets ObjectMapper visibility. This makes serialization deterministic regardless of what third-party libraries do to the ObjectMapper. Generate this class once when creating the project structure:

If a REST endpoint returns objects with only boolean fields (e.g. only `overdue: false` when the model has many more fields), the root cause is almost always ObjectMapper visibility being overridden. Fix by generating the JacksonConfig class above — do not patch individual model classes with @JsonProperty annotations.
